key_points: Limited direct student feedback on dorms in available data
Campus integration into historic downtown is a noted perk
Dorm quality comparisons to UofSC were a decision factor
key_quotes: Anyone have insight as to which school is better in terms of DORMS
The campus is integrated into the city in a picturesque way

key_points: Greek life is only ~22%, easy to exist outside it
Gender imbalance is real: only 33% male student body
Gay male community described as 'incredibly small and isolating'
key_quotes: College of Charleston is only about 22% Greek
Only 33% of the school is males so that makes the population so much smaller
The community is incredibly small and isolating here

key_points: No direct student comments about dining quality or options
Downtown Charleston location means off-campus food options are plentiful
Cannot score fairly without student dining hall feedback
key_quotes: A good place to hang out is downtown by the pier
There is an ice cream shop right by the strip that is fire

key_points: One parent reported child came home from freshman year with PTSD
No institutional mental health support resources mentioned by students
Stress around COVID cancellations and poor communication noted
key_quotes: My kid came from her freshman year with PTSD

key_points: Merit scholarships available and frequently mentioned as a draw
Honors College fellowship program can significantly increase aid
Out-of-state aid around $12-16k/year reported by admitted students

key_points: Described as better for undergrad opportunities than UofSC by students
Weak resources for niche majors like animation and some STEM fields
Honors College widely praised; stronger academics than general admits
key_quotes: CofC has better opportunities for undergraduates. You will not regret it

key_points: COVID communication was vague and frustrating to students
Portal instructions for withdrawal are confusing or nonexistent
Transfer acceptance rate is high (93%) suggesting accessible admissions

key_points: Campus is consistently called beautiful and picturesque by students and visitors
Historic downtown Charleston fully integrated into campus experience
Actual beach is farther from campus than students sometimes expect
key_quotes: This college is picturesque, that's the only word for it
The way the campus is integrated into the city
I am aware CofC is farther from its beach than UNC Wilmington

key_points: No strong alumni network or career placement data mentioned by students
Seen mostly as a safety or target school, not a career launchpad
Pre-law program noted as a positive by one military student commenter

Their pre-law program is also strong
Clemson and Charleston are pretty similar academically

key_points: In-state with merit aid or Honors scholarship makes it genuinely cheap
Out-of-state without strong aid package considered poor value by students
Multiple students chose it over debt at bigger-name schools

Not worth taking 160k in debt for a school like UGA. Go to COC
I would go to the one with the best deal

key_points: Fun, beach-adjacent social school with a strong aesthetic appeal
Frequently listed as a safety but students genuinely enjoy it
Better fit for students who want city life over traditional college town
key_quotes: College of Charleston probably checks off more of your priorities than any other

do NOT go to cofc
